question: Will France be in the top 5 at Eurovision 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 5 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 5 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

events.title: Eurovision 2026: Top 5
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 5 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 5 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors perennial powerhouses like Sweden (65% implied probability for top 5) and Italy (55%), propelled by their dominant recent track records—Sweden's back-to-back wins in 2023 and runner-up in 2024—bolstered by rigorous national selections like Melodifestivalen. Eurovision 2025 in Basel (May 13-17) looms as the pivotal near-term catalyst, as its winner secures 2026 hosting rights and traditional home-field advantage, potentially elevating underdogs like Croatia or France. Low-volume early markets hinge on historical televote-jury splits and EBU rules, with national final announcements expected fall 2025; monitor for breakout artist reveals that could swing odds.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 10 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 Top 10 market heavily favors perennial contenders like Sweden (25% implied probability for top 10), Italy, and Ukraine, driven by their consistent strong finishes—Sweden's seven wins and Italy's recent dominance—while newcomer buzz around the UK lingers from 2024's runner-up surge. Recent national selection announcements for Eurovision 2025, set for May in Basel, Switzerland, add volatility, as the 2025 winner will host 2026, potentially boosting their odds via home-crowd televote edge. Traders eye late-2025 preselections across 40+ countries for breakout acts, with jury-televote splits historically amplifying fan favorites; watch ESC 2025 results on May 17 for momentum shifts in these early markets.

question: Will Tesla sell a Cybercab for 30k or less in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla sells at least one Cybercab vehicle to a qualifying retail customer for a base purchase price of $30,000 USD or less by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

“Cybercab” refers to the specific autonomous vehicle unveiled by Tesla in October 2024 under the Cybercab name, or a clearly designated successor product marketed by Tesla as the same vehicle model.

A qualifying retail customer must be a member of the general public purchasing the vehicle in a bona fide retail transaction under publicly available terms. Sales to employees, executives, family members, subsidiaries, internal entities, or for promotional, testing, or internal fleet purposes will not qualify.

The base purchase price refers to the vehicle’s listed retail price before taxes, registration fees, delivery fees, financing costs, or third-party incentives. Official Tesla discounts or manufacturer incentives count toward the base price.

Preorders, deposits, announced pricing targets, prototype deliveries, or internal fleet deployments will not qualify unless a completed retail sale meeting the above criteria occurs.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Tesla, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Tesla sell a Cybercab for 30k or less in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla sells at least one Cybercab vehicle to a qualifying retail customer for a base purchase price of $30,000 USD or less by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

“Cybercab” refers to the specific autonomous vehicle unveiled by Tesla in October 2024 under the Cybercab name, or a clearly designated successor product marketed by Tesla as the same vehicle model.

A qualifying retail customer must be a member of the general public purchasing the vehicle in a bona fide retail transaction under publicly available terms. Sales to employees, executives, family members, subsidiaries, internal entities, or for promotional, testing, or internal fleet purposes will not qualify.

The base purchase price refers to the vehicle’s listed retail price before taxes, registration fees, delivery fees, financing costs, or third-party incentives. Official Tesla discounts or manufacturer incentives count toward the base price.

Preorders, deposits, announced pricing targets, prototype deliveries, or internal fleet deployments will not qualify unless a completed retail sale meeting the above criteria occurs.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Tesla, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism, fueled by Tesla's history of delayed timelines and upward price revisions on products like the Cybertruck—which launched years late at over twice the initial $40,000 target—drives the 72% market-implied probability against Cybercab sales at $30,000 or less in 2026. Elon Musk reaffirmed the sub-$30k pricing goal during the October 10 "We, Robot" unveiling and Q3 earnings call, targeting volume production in 2026 ahead of unsupervised Full Self-Driving rollout. However, traders highlight execution risks, including regulatory approvals for robotaxi operations, supply chain hurdles for the steerless design, and autonomy hardware costs, with no confirmed sales contracts yet and historical precedents tempering optimism ahead of Q4 earnings.

question: Will Lionel Messi win the 2026 MLS Golden Boot?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Golden Boot.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: MLS: 2026 Golden Boot Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Golden Boot.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lionel Messi's unmatched scoring prowess and Inter Miami contract extension through 2026 drive his dominant 32.5% implied probability as traders' top 2026 MLS Golden Boot pick, fueled by his league-record 20 goals in 19 regular-season starts this year despite Copa América and injury absences. Supporting challengers reflect current form: Vancouver's Brian White (6.8%) with 18 goals amid playoff push, Colorado Rapids' breakout star Philip Zinckernagel (6.3%) in their surprise West lead, and LA Galaxy's Dejan Joveljić (5.9%) riding 16 tallies and postseason momentum. Key factors include team attacking depth, home-field edges, and durability risks, with recent playoff results and potential winter transfers adding volatility to long-range projections.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year remains tightly bunched among Yohei Takaoka, Michael Collodi, Luis Barraza, and Joe Willis, reflecting the league's deep parity at the position after several keepers delivered elite 2024 campaigns in save percentage, clean sheets, and goals against average. Takaoka's league-leading shutouts for Chicago Fire, Collodi's late-season emergence with San Jose Earthquakes, Barraza's reliability for New England Revolution, and Willis's veteran steadiness anchoring Nashville SC have traders projecting sustained excellence amid team upgrades like Charlotte FC's support for Kristijan Kahlina. No single goalkeeper dominates due to MLS volatility—playoff runs, penalty heroics, and distribution improvements can swing awards—keeping implied probabilities clustered in the high 20s to low 30s as offseason extensions bolster contenders.

This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year remains tightly bunched among Yohei Takaoka, Michael Collodi, Luis Barraza, and Joe Willis, reflecting the league's deep parity at the position after several keepers delivered elite 2024 campaigns in save percentage, clean sheets, and goals against average. Takaoka's league-leading shutouts for Chicago Fire, Collodi's late-season emergence with San Jose Earthquakes, Barraza's reliability for New England Revolution, and Willis's veteran steadiness anchoring Nashville SC have traders projecting sustained excellence amid team upgrades like Charlotte FC's support for Kristijan Kahlina. No single goalkeeper dominates due to MLS volatility—playoff runs, penalty heroics, and distribution improvements can swing awards—keeping implied probabilities clustered in the high 20s to low 30s as offseason extensions bolster contenders.

question: Predict.fun FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Predict.fun's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Predict.fun (https://predict.fun/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by December 31?
description:
If Reza Pahlavi visits Iran between market creation and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Reza Pahlavi physically entering the terrestrial territory of Iran. Whether or not Reza Pahlavi enters Iranian airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies low odds for Reza Pahlavi entering Iran by the market deadline, driven by the exiled son's lack of announced travel plans amid the regime's longstanding arrest warrant against him and severe risks for opposition figures. Recent developments include Pahlavi's July 2024 meetings with Israeli leaders and calls for Iranian uprising from abroad, amplifying his symbolic role in protest movements like Woman, Life, Freedom, but without feasible entry pathways under current border controls. No confirmed diplomatic shifts or safe passage negotiations exist, though escalating domestic unrest or international sanctions could alter dynamics; watch for regime announcements or Pahlavi statements ahead of key protest anniversaries.

question: Will Boston Breach win DreamHack Major 2?

question: US forces enter Iran by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US forces enter Iran by..?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on US forces entering Iran shows low implied probabilities, anchored by Washington's firm policy against direct ground intervention amid the Israel-Iran aerial exchanges. Israel's limited October 26 airstrikes on Iranian military sites—retaliating for Tehran's October 1 missile attack—drew defensive US backing via naval assets but no offensive escalation, as President Biden reiterated de-escalation commitments. No troop deployments or mobilization orders have emerged from the Pentagon. The November 5 US presidential election represents the pivotal upcoming event, with a potential policy shift under new leadership, while Iran's nuclear program reviews by the IAEA add monitoring focus. Historical US aversion to Iran invasion sustains trader caution.

question: Iran strike on US military by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran carries out a military strike against the U.S. military, inclusive of all U.S. military ships, planes, bases and other infrastructure, between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Iranian missile, drone, or other strikes which are shot down or miss their targets will not be considered for this market. Strikes on U.S. military unmanned aerial vehicles/projectiles and U.S. commercial/civilian ships will NOT count toward a "Yes" resolution.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks on the U.S. military by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source for this market is official statements from the U.S. and Iranian governments, however a consensus of credible may also be used.

question: Will the U.S. invade Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 82.3% implied probability on "No" for a U.S. invasion of Iran by March 31 stems primarily from the absence of any official U.S. military mobilization, White House announcements, or congressional authorizations signaling such action. Recent developments reinforce this: the Biden administration has prioritized targeted airstrikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq, Syria, and Yemen's Houthis, alongside sanctions and IAEA diplomacy on Iran's nuclear program, without escalating to direct invasion threats. Israel's limited strikes on Iranian targets in October 2024 elicited restrained U.S. support focused on defense, not offense. Post-election transition adds uncertainty, but historical U.S. reluctance for major ground wars post-Afghanistan shapes trader consensus amid ongoing proxy tensions.

question: Will Dejan Joveljic win 2026 MLS Most Valuable Player?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Landon Donovan Most Valuable Player.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Shohei Ohtani dominates trader sentiment at 41.5% implied probability for 2026 NL MVP, driven by his two-way dominance, the Dodgers' World Series pedigree, and projected full pitching return after elbow surgery, positioning L.A. as perennial contenders. Juan Soto's 12.5% holds amid his recent $765 million Yankees pact shifting him to the AL, likely reflecting slow market adjustment or slim trade speculation rather than realistic NL eligibility. Mookie Betts (12.3%) rides Dodgers lineup synergy, while Ronald Acuña Jr. (11%) eyes post-ACL rebound with Atlanta's core. Rising stars Elly De La Cruz and Fernando Tatis Jr. draw bets on speed-power upside, as winter meetings signings underscore divisional arms races ahead of spring training.
